Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
All laboratory values are within normal limits except for a mildly elevated chloride, which is likely of limited clinical significance in the absence of other electrolyte disturbances or acid-base abnormalities. Renal function, glycemic control, and lipid profile are unremarkable. No evidence of microalbuminuria. Overall, results are stable and do not indicate acute pathology.
